Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Additive manufacturing Ti-22Al-25 Nb alloy with excellent high temperature tensile properties by electron beam powder bed fusion

Composition_Info.Note: Balance Ti calculated from measured values; Powder nominal: Al 23.60, O 0.11, Nb 26.10, N <0.04, H <0.004 (at%)

Process_Text.original: Laser beam powder bed fusion (PBF-LB) using Renishaw AM-400 system; layer rotation 67°; Power 140 W; scanning speed 0.6 m/s; hatch distance 0.12 mm; layer thickness 30 μm; pre-heating temperature 170°C (maximum device limit); beam diameter 70 μm (FWHM)
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-LB with 140 W laser power, 0.6 m/s scanning speed, 30 μm layer thickness, 170°C pre-heating

Microstructure_Text.original: Fine columnar β-phase grains with epitaxial growth along building direction; O phase (7.34% area fraction) dispersed in β matrix; β-phase grains tend to grow along (101) and (111) directions; high cooling rate (10^5-10^8 K/s) caused grain refinement; stronger texture intensity compared to PBF-EB samples
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar β-phase grains with epitaxial growth; 7.34% O-phase area fraction; grains grow along (101) and (111) directions

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.XRD: Bruker D8 Advance, Cu-Kα radiation, 30°-90° 2θ range; O and β phases identified, no α2 phase
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM: Zeiss Sigma 300; chemical etching (1 mL HF: 3 mL HNO3: 10 mL H2O) for 100s
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Tecnai G2 F30 with SAED and EDX analysis
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EBSD: Bruker e-FlashFS with Esprit software, 0.1 μm step size

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties in horizontal direction

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties in horizontal direction

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; 15 min soak time before testing; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: High-temperature tensile properties; rapid decrease at 650°C; brittle fracture observed

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties; high ductility with dimple fracture morphology

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; 15 min soak time before testing; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: High-temperature tensile properties; near-zero elongation at 650°C; brittle intergranular fracture

Composition_Info.Note: Balance Ti calculated from measured values; Powder nominal: Al 23.60, O 0.11, Nb 26.10, N <0.04, H <0.004 (at%); Significant Al loss due to higher pre-heating temperature

Process_Text.original: Electron beam powder bed fusion (PBF-EB) using Sailong Y150 system; high pre-heating temperature of 830°C (near initial precipitation temperature of O phase); conventional 90° layer rotation; Power 1050 W; scanning speed 5.8 m/s; hatch distance 0.11 mm; layer thickness 50 μm; beam diameter 220 μm (FWHM)
Process_Text.simplified: PBF-EB with 1050 W beam power, 5.8 m/s scanning speed, 50 μm layer thickness, 830°C high pre-heating temperature

Microstructure_Text.original: Equiaxed β-phase grains with uniform distribution; high area fraction of O phase (42.3%, approximately 6× higher than PBF-LB); β-phase grains grow along (001) direction; lower texture intensity; precipitation and growth of O phase inhibited epitaxial growth of β-phase grains; weaker texture intensity favorable for homogenization of tensile properties
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Equiaxed β-phase grains with uniform distribution; 42.3% O-phase area fraction (6× higher than PBF-LB); grains grow along (001) direction; uniform grain morphology

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.XRD: Bruker D8 Advance, Cu-Kα radiation, 30°-90° 2θ range; O and β phases identified, no α2 phase; increased O-phase peaks compared to PBF-LB
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM: Zeiss Sigma 300; chemical etching (1 mL HF: 3 mL HNO3: 10 mL H2O) for 100s
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Tecnai G2 F30 with SAED and EDX analysis; Al enriched in O phase, Nb enriched in β phase
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EBSD: Bruker e-FlashFS with Esprit software, 0.1 μm step size

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties; poor elongation at RT with brittle fracture; traces of strip-shaped O phase peeling observed

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; 15 min soak time before testing; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Excellent high-temperature tensile strength at 650°C; breakthrough improvement for TiAl alloys by AM

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties; excellent UTS but poor elongation; brittle fracture morphology

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; 15 min soak time before testing; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Excellent high-temperature tensile strength at 650°C (803 MPa); transgranular fracture mechanism; breakthrough for TiAl AM alloys

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K (RT); strain rate 2.5×10^-4 s^-1; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: RT tensile properties; low ductility; brittle fracture

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 923.15 K (650°C); strain rate 1×10^-3 s^-1; 15 min soak time before testing; horizontal direction
Properties_Info.Note: Improved ductility at 650°C with dimples on fracture surface; transgranular fracture mechanism
